subject: Public Health
title: Screening Programs
description: A micro-lesson that makes Screening Programs usable.

hook.text: Catching cancer before symptoms appear can mean the difference between a simple treatment and a death sentence.

buildup.text: Screening tests—mammograms, Pap smears, colonoscopies—detect disease early when treatment is most effective.

discovery.text: Cervical cancer screening has reduced deaths by 80% in countries with organized programs—early detection saves lives.

twist.text: Not all screening is beneficial—overdiagnosis can lead to unnecessary treatment, anxiety, and harm.

climax.text: Effective screening programs balance benefits and harms—targeting the right disease, age group, and test interval.

punchline.text: Finding disease early is powerful—but only when finding it changes the outcome.

quiz.question: By how much has cervical cancer screening reduced deaths in countries with programs?
